req.title: Manager creates performance-review
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a performance-review through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path creation by an authenticated manager with all mandatory fields (EP – valid partition); (2) missing mandatory field producing a 400 (NEG); (3) unauthenticated request producing a 401 (NEG/DT); (4) forbidden role producing a 403 (DT); (5) boundary on the employee_name field at its maximum allowed length (BVA); (6) state-transition check that a created review can be fetched and has an audit record (STC). Together these cover the positive, negative, and boundary obligations and all four error pathways stated in the requirement.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_review_all_mandatory_fields
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <manager_auth_fixture>
POST /api/performance-reviews with all mandatory fields populated
expected: Response body contains a non-empty 'id' field
Response body reflects the submitted mandatory fields
trace: R-WEB-021: authenticated manager can create a performance-review
R-WEB-021: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_manager_creates_review_all_mandatory_fields(manager_auth_fixture, api_base_url):
    token = manager_auth_fixture()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "<valid_employee_id>",
        "employee_name": "Jane Doe",
        "review_period": "2024-Q1",
        "rating": 3,
        "reviewer_id": "<valid_reviewer_id>"
    }
    response = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/api/performance-reviews", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "id" in body and body["id"], "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"
    assert body.get("employee_id") == payload["employee_id"]
    assert body.get("review_period") == payload["review_period"]

tests.name: test_create_review_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <manager_auth_fixture>
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing the missing field
trace: R-WEB-021: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-021: error pathway – 400 on validation error
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_review_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(manager_auth_fixture, api_base_url):
    token = manager_auth_fixture()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "<valid_employee_id>",
        "employee_name": "Jane Doe",
        "review_period": "2024-Q1"
        # 'rating' intentionally omitted
    }
    response = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/api/performance-reviews", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "rating" in error_text or "validation" in error_text, \
        "Error body must reference the missing field or validation failure"

tests.name: test_create_review_unauthenticated_returns_401
actions: POST /api/performance-reviews with all mandatory fields but without an Authorization header
expected: Response body indicates an authentication error

tests.name: test_create_review_forbidden_role_returns_403
actions: Obtain a valid employee (non-manager) auth token via <employee_auth_fixture>
trace: R-WEB-021: error pathway – 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_review_forbidden_role_returns_403(employee_auth_fixture, api_base_url):
    token = employee_auth_fixture()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "<valid_employee_id>",
        "employee_name": "Jane Doe",
        "review_period": "2024-Q1",
        "rating": 3,
        "reviewer_id": "<valid_reviewer_id>"
    }
    response = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/api/performance-reviews", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {response.status_code}"

tests.name: test_create_review_employee_name_at_max_length
preconditions: API spec defines employee_name max length as 255 characters
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <manager_auth_fixture>
POST /api/performance-reviews with employee_name set to exactly 255 characters
expected: Response body contains a non-empty 'id' field
Response body reflects employee_name of 255 characters
trace: R-WEB-021: mandatory fields must be validated (boundary of field length)
R-WEB-021: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

MAX_NAME_LEN = 255

def test_create_review_employee_name_at_max_length(manager_auth_fixture, api_base_url):
    token = manager_auth_fixture()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    long_name = "A" * MAX_NAME_LEN
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "<valid_employee_id>",
        "employee_name": long_name,
        "review_period": "2024-Q1",
        "rating": 3,
        "reviewer_id": "<valid_reviewer_id>"
    }
    response = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/api/performance-reviews", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "id" in body and body["id"], "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"
    assert body.get("employee_name") == long_name

tests.name: test_create_review_audit_record_written_on_success
preconditions: audit log endpoint /api/audit-logs is accessible to the manager
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <manager_auth_fixture>
Capture the new review id from the response
expected: HTTP status of POST is 200 or 201
Audit log GET returns at least one record with matching entity_id
Audit record action field indicates a create event
trace: R-WEB-021: audit record written on success
R-WEB-021: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_review_audit_record_written_on_success(manager_auth_fixture, api_base_url):
    token = manager_auth_fixture()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "employee_id": "<valid_employee_id>",
        "employee_name": "Audit Test User",
        "review_period": "2024-Q2",
        "rating": 4,
        "reviewer_id": "<valid_reviewer_id>"
    }
    create_resp = requests.post(f"{api_base_url}/api/performance-reviews", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert create_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    entity_id = create_resp.json()["id"]
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/audit-logs",
        params={"entity_type": "performance-review", "entity_id": entity_id},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    records = audit_resp.json()
    assert len(records) >= 1, "At least one audit record must exist for the new review"
    actions = [r.get("action", "").lower() for r in records]
    assert any("create" in a for a in actions), "Audit record must indicate a create action"
